scope: CHANGE-0006 / SPEC-0012 working-tree diff on feat/change-0006-state-cli (worktree /Users/ales/Projects/aai-feat-change-0006-state-cli)

findings.message: reset-block destroys |- (and |+/>+) style notes: else-branch replaces the whole field span, deleting prior audit-note lines (reproduced). CLI-emitted >- style unaffected; hand-edited/legacy files at risk.

Inline top-level block header (metrics: {}, metrics: null) treated as block header: append-run/set-* splice nested lines under it producing invalid YAML with exit 0; check-state.mjs (dup-key-only) passes the broken file (reproduced).

Plain-scalar values written verbatim without quoting/validation: set-validation --ref 'CHANGE-1: bad' (also --branch/--base-ref/--model/--test-id) yields invalid YAML with exit 0 that the validator cannot detect (reproduced).

Concurrent invocations lose updates (read-modify-write, rename-last-wins; reproduced). No lock taken (RFC-0004 docs-lock unused) and the last-writer-wins/single-writer posture is documented nowhere in state.mjs or SPEC-0012; role prompts unconditionally instruct per-role append-run, tension with SUBAGENT_PROTOCOL sole-writer rule under parallel dispatch.
Unknown/misspelled flags silently ignored: set-validation --evidnce/--notse drops evidence and notes with exit 0 (reproduced) — contrary to the closed-set fail-loud posture the CLI exists to enforce.
set-focus leaves the previous scope's current_focus.spec_path stale when --spec-path not passed (no consumer found today).
CRLF input file is rewritten whole-file LF on any mutation (documented normalization); TEST-020 diff locality does not hold for CRLF checkouts.
log-tick on a duplicate-key STATE reads the FIRST duplicate block; YAML last-key-wins would give the LAST — *_after tick fields may reflect the dropped block (corrupt input only).
--state/--ticks given without a value silently fall back to the REAL default runtime paths; a fixture invocation that drops the argument mutates live state with exit 0.
Primary-path reset via set-validation --status not_run self-stamps run_at_utc (never null), so the documented default shape still needs the prescribed follow-up hand edit.
Filesystem write failures surface as uncaught exceptions with exit 1, numerically colliding with the integrity-refusal exit-1 contract (message text disambiguates; target never corrupted).
Negative integers accepted (--tokens-in -5, --tick -1); stale .tmp-<pid> files never reaped; set-phase field insert can land after a trailing blank line (valid, cosmetic).

hostile_experiments: |- notes + reset-block -> prior note lines deleted (W1)
metrics: {} + append-run -> invalid YAML, exit 0, check-state OK (W2)
two concurrent mutators -> one committed mutation lost (W4)
status 'fail  # comment' + reset-block -> safe refusal exit 2 (clean)
CRLF file -> LF whole-file rewrite, exit 0 (I2, documented)
duplicate-key STATE + log-tick -> tolerated, first-block read (I3)
column-0 'metrics:' inside folded note -> not constructible (textFieldLines re-indents; clean)
notes: 0 ERROR findings; Stage 1 fully compliant. WARNINGs are hostile/non-canonical-input robustness + concurrency-posture documentation gaps; recommend a follow-up hardening work item for W1-W5.
